Author: AemiliusXIV
Name: Orchestrion Aria
Punchline: BGM replacement with local audio support
Description:
Play and replace FFXIV background music. Supports local MP3/WAV files from your PC alongside all in-game tracks, with fade transitions and volume sync.

Originally by perchbird & Meli: https://github.com/perchbirdd/OrchestrionPlugin
Changelog:
Local songs now appear in the History tab. Anything you played from your own
library is as easy to find again as a game track. If you'd rather keep history
limited to game BGM, there's a toggle for it in Local Library settings.

You can scrub local files now. Click anywhere on the player's progress bar to
jump to that point in the track. Game BGM has no position we can read, so only
local audio responds to clicks.

Last, an optional now-playing toast. When a song starts it flashes a short note
on screen that fades by itself and leaves nothing in chat. Off by default; turn
it on under General settings.

Author: AemiliusXIV
Name: Party Sorter
Punchline: Drag party members to reorder your party list.
Description: Hold Ctrl (configurable) and drag a party member onto another to reorder. Saves each group's preferred order and automatically restores it when the same members meet again — perfect for statics that want a consistent layout every pull. Supports per-group auto-apply modes, locking saved orders against accidental overwrites, and per-duty separate orders. Commands: /psorter (open settings), /psorter save (save current order to chat).

Author: AemiliusXIV
Name: ChronoLog
Punchline: OBS chapter markers and YouTube timestamps for every raid pull. Use /chrono.
Description:
Tracks pulls, wipes and clears in any duty. Per pull: fight name, phase, boss HP%, first death, duration.

Outputs OBS chapter markers via websocket and a paste-ready YouTube description block. A direct API push to a live stream's description is planned for a later release.

Short resets are filtered out automatically. /chrono to open, /chrono cfg for settings.
Changelog: Primary command renamed to /chrono (/clog conflicted with the in-game crafting log). Short wipes under 15 s now appear as dimmed 'Brief attempt' rows - excluded from the text export but counted so pull numbering stays in sync with FFLogs. OBS no longer attempts reconnect when OBS itself exits cleanly. Fixed a rare case where a session could split into two separate entries after a plugin reload mid-raid.
